summary: Excellent soundboard mix captures a fiery show highlighted by a scorching La Bamba and a powerhouse second set.
review: The Healy‑engineered soundboard delivers a clear, well‑balanced mix with only brief drop‑outs on “Stranger.”  Musically the concert is electric – Jerry’s Spanish‑language solo on “La Bamba,” Bobby’s high‑energy vocals on “Desolation Row” and “Good Lovin’,” plus a thunderous second set make this one of the most memorable ’87 performances.

ai_rating.rationale: Four detailed reviews are overwhelmingly positive about both performance and recording; only a single moderate‑rated review notes minor technical hiccups, resulting in a high overall rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Healy mix is clear and instruments sit nicely in the mix; occasional technical difficulty/drop‑out on “Stranger” but otherwise crisp and listenable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Set 1 starts a bit weak on lyrics but builds into strong guitar work; Set 2 is powerful and tight, with seamless energy despite some non‑musical gaps noted by one reviewer.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Multiple reviewers praise his scorching solos, especially the Spanish vocal solo on “La Bamba,” noting he was ‘on fire’ after recent health issues.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bobby (Brent) is described as ‘on fire,’ delivering high‑energy vocals and driving the show’s intensity throughout.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keyboardist Brent Mydland receives acclaim for being at his peak, adding vibrant texture to the performance.
